data.title: Economist Rosenberg dismisses BEA's reported 4.3% GDP growth, calculating true growth at 0.8%, citing underlying weaknesses masked by government spending.
data.description: The U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis reported a 4.3% annual increase in third-quarter real GDP, but economist David Rosenberg believes this number is misleading. He argues that true growth is only at 0.8% due to government spending and depleted savings masking economic weakness. The increase in GDP was primarily driven by consumer spending, exports, and government spending, but Rosenberg claims that these figures are manipulated. Once government spending, shifting import data, and a decrease in personal savings rate are removed, the economy shows minimal growth. Rosenberg specifically points out flat personal disposable income growth as a red flag contradicting the apparent consumption boom.

data.title: CB&I acquiring Petrofac's Asset Solutions business to diversify, grow, and expand geographically. Dec 24, 2025.
data.description: CB&I, based in The Woodlands, Texas, declared on December 24, 2025, its plans to acquire Petrofac's Asset Solutions business to enhance its business diversification, growth prospects, and geographic presence. data.title: Sanofi to buy Dynavax, gaining adult hepatitis B vaccine and shingles drug in pipeline.
data.description: Pharmaceutical company Sanofi has announced plans to acquire Dynavax. This acquisition will bring into Sanofi's portfolio a marketed adult hepatitis B vaccine and a phase 1/2 shingles candidate. This move is part of Sanofi's strategy to expand its offerings. The deal involves a significant investment, highlighting Sanofi's commitment to growth and diversification in the pharmaceutical industry.

data.title: Galaxy Digital Inc. CEO Mike Novogratz shares positive outlook on Bitcoin, not giving up despite negative sentiment. Believes Bitcoin hasn't hit all-time high yet, suggests waiting for $100,000 before turning bullish. Emphasizes Bitcoin's strong support system and belief in its potential.
data.description: CEO of Galaxy Digital Inc. (NASDAQ: GLXY), Mike Novogratz, shared in an interview that he remains optimistic about Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) despite prevailing negativity, but advised caution against premature bullishness. Novogratz, speaking with Anthony Scaramucci, founder of SkyBridge Capital, conveyed his belief that Bitcoin has not reached its peak value yet, acknowledging the need for further recovery. He emphasized the importance of crypto pricing momentum, citing the significance of narrative in driving prices. Novogratz suggested that a bullish stance should wait until Bitcoin surpasses $100,000, highlighting the enduring support for Bitcoin through a robust infrastructure and belief system. The full article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
